description: This plot presents the variation of work hardening rate (WHR, measured between 1% and 1.5% plastic strain) of as-cast or annealed polycrystalline Fe-Al alloys as a function of Al content (0 to 40 at.%), including data from prior literature and the present study. Black circles represent binary Fe-Al alloys, while two separate data points correspond to Fe-Al alloys doped with ~0.1 at.% B. For binary Fe-Al, WHR stays below 2 GPa when Al content is lower than 20 at.%, rises sharply to a peak of ~16 GPa at 28–29 at.% Al, then decreases to ~5–6 GPa when Al content exceeds 35 at.%. Addition of 0.1 at.% B shows no consistent significant effect on WHR. This figure supports the core conclusion that ordered Fe-Al alloys exhibit higher WHR, with the sharp peak at 26–29 at.% Al attributed to hardening mechanisms related to superdislocations, superdislocation dipoles and sessile dislocation reactions in ordered phases.
